name: anonymous user at turn 7 — soft signup directive present but NO tool fire
description: Soft-signup window (turns 5–9). The SIGNUP_SOFT directive instructs the model to mention sign-in once per session at a natural pause, in text — NOT to fire the `auth_user` tool on this state. Tool firing is reserved for `signup_hard` (turn 10+) when the user says something persistence-adjacent. This fixture pins the boundary: even on a slightly persistence-adjacent message at turn 7, the agent stays in text mode and does NOT fire `auth_user`. Catches a regression where the soft directive accidentally inherits the hard directive's tool-firing rule.

parts.text: what's a good way to wind down in the evening?
